idea.hook: Want to visit our nearest star, Proxima Centauri? It's closer than you think, but...
facts: Proxima Centauri is 'just' 4.24 light-years away from us.
Our fastest spacecraft ever, Voyager 1 (traveling ~17 km/s), would take ~75,000 years to reach Proxima Centauri.
Even a hypothetical future probe traveling at 10% the speed of light (0.1c) would still need 42.4 years for the one-way trip.
idea.payoff: Interstellar travel demands an unimaginable technological leap, making even our closest stellar neighbor a multi-generational journey!
idea.title: Proxima Centauri: A Lifetime Journey! 🤯🚀

scenes.visual: A stylized Earth with a faint starfield background. A question mark subtly appears.
Two glowing dots representing Sun/Earth and Proxima Centauri, connected by a shimmering distance line against a deep space background.
An animated Voyager 1 probe flying slowly across the screen, leaving a very long, faint trajectory line behind it, emphasizing immense distance/time.

scenes.text: Even at 10% light speed (0.1c), it's a 42.4-year trip!
scenes.visual: A sleek, futuristic, faster-than-light-looking probe streaks across the screen, still with a significant distance ahead, conveying speed vs. scale.

scenes.text: Interstellar travel demands an unimaginable technological leap. A multi-generational journey!
scenes.visual: A breathtaking view of a distant, bright star (Proxima Centauri) slowly rotating, emphasizing its beauty and unreachable distance. Text appears over it.

script.thumbnail_text: Proxima Centauri: Closer Yet So Far!
